title: Barter Inflation
description: Piglins gradually demand more gold as players trade with them, creating a dynamic nether economy that resets on full moons.
body:
# Barter Inflation

This datapack introduces an event-driven economy system to piglin bartering in the Nether. Every successful barter transaction logged in the world increases a global inflation counter. As inflation rises, piglins become pickier, they occasionally reject gold ingots entirely, snorting dismissively and keeping the gold without giving anything back.

## Inflation Levels

- **Low inflation (0-20 trades):** Bartering works normally
- **Medium inflation (21-50):** 15% chance of rejection
- **High inflation (51-100):** 35% rejection chance, piglins may demand multiple ingots before accepting

## Economy Reset

When a full moon rises in the Overworld, the economy resets and inflation drops to zero, and piglins become generous traders again. A subtle particle effect appears around piglins during high inflation periods to warn players.

## Technical Details

The pack tracks all statistics using scoreboards and runs checks only when barter events occur, keeping performance impact minimal. No commands are required for players -- everything operates automatically through entity detection and game event triggers.

Server operators can check current inflation levels with:
```
/scoreboard players get #inflation barter.economy
```
